summary: Verified the ZERO USER CALL CoinGecko cache architecture end-to-end. Backend pytest: 28/28 PASSED covering /api/health, /api/auth/login (super admin), /api/crypto/prices (20 coins, cached=True, real BTC>$10k), /api/crypto/chart/{bitcoin,ethereum,solana,dogecoin}?days={1,7,30,90,365} with the expected point counts (1d≈24, 7d≈169, 30d≈721, 90d≈2167, 365d≈366), /api/crypto/rainbow (437 points, current_band_label, current_price), /api/crypto/global, /api/crypto/trending (15 coins). Backend logs prove ZERO user-triggered CoinGecko calls — all pro-api.coingecko.com hits are emitted by the scheduler (_refresh_prices_and_sparklines every 120s, _prefetch_long_charts hourly, _prefetch_rainbow hourly). User requests to /api/crypto/chart/* and /api/crypto/rainbow show NO corresponding httpx call. Frontend Playwright: homepage loads (Mentova logo, marketing carousel, Get Started + Sign In CTAs). Sign-In flow with super admin (jcuradeau.7@gmail.com / Crypto2026!) works (POST /api/auth/login → 200, access_token returned). Post-login dashboard renders crypto cards (BTC $59,113 -2.77%, ETH $1,560 -4.76%, USDT $1.00, Global $2.13T). 'Market' tab renders the full Crypto Market view: Global Market ($2.13T market cap, $112.36B 24h volume, BTC 55.8% / ETH 8.9% dominance bar) and Top Cryptocurrencies list with 9+ rows including Bitcoin, Ethereum, Tether, BNB, USDC, XRP, Solana, TRON, Figure Heloc with mini-sparklines. The frontend made GET /api/crypto/prices and /api/crypto/global → both 200.

minor.issue: During the ~90s cold-start window after a backend restart, the endpoint returns success=True with data=[] for coins whose long-period prefetch is still pending (only ~7 of 20 coins are prefetched per minute). UX-wise this looks identical to a successful empty chart. Rainbow correctly returns success=False with error='Rainbow chart loading — please retry in a moment'; the chart endpoint should adopt the same pattern (e.g. success=False, error='Chart loading — please retry in a moment') instead of an empty success=True payload.

issues: React Native Web emits many warnings: shadow* deprecated → use boxShadow; animationName invalid → use animationKeyframes; useNativeDriver fallback. Cosmetic, not blocking, but noisy.

action_items: (Minor) Change /api/crypto/chart endpoint to return success=False + error='Chart loading — please retry in a moment' when the requested period's cache slot is empty, matching the existing pattern in /api/crypto/rainbow. This avoids the rare cold-start window where a few coins return success=True with data=[].
(Cosmetic) Migrate residual RN style props (shadow*, animationName) to web-compatible boxShadow / animationKeyframes to silence the console warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py _coingecko_scheduler architecture is solid: 3 independent loops (120s prices+sparklines, 300s global, 600s trending, hourly long charts + rainbow). The chart endpoint at ~L930 truly never calls CoinGecko — verified by httpx log grep over 5 minutes of user traffic.

Cold-start UX: long-period prefetch is sequential (~3 requests/coin × 20 coins ≈ 90s). During that window the chart endpoint silently returns empty data for coins not yet prefetched. Either (a) seed an in-progress flag and return success=False+error='Chart loading…', or (b) parallelize the prefetch with a small bounded concurrency (e.g. 4) so the cold-start window drops to ~25s.

Sparkline-derived 1d/7d charts correctly have no volume field (CoinGecko sparkline payload is price-only). The frontend tolerates missing volume. If the chart UI later needs volume for 7d, it must call a different prefetched payload.

Rainbow chart cold-start handled correctly: success=False + error='Rainbow chart loading — please retry in a moment' until _prefetch_rainbow populates _rainbow_cache. This is the pattern to mirror in the chart endpoint.
test_credentials: Super Admin: jcuradeau.7@gmail.com / Crypto2026! (worked for /api/auth/login and frontend Sign In)
seed_data_creation: None — all tests are read-only against shared cache.
context_for_next_testing_agent: If you observe transient failures on /api/crypto/chart/* with success=True but data=[] or on /api/crypto/rainbow returning success=False, that usually means the backend was restarted recently and the long-period prefetch (~90s for 20 coins) hasn't finished. Wait ~90s and re-run. Tail /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log for the lines 'Long-period charts pre-fetched for 20 coins' and 'Rainbow pre-fetched: 437 pts, band: <label>' — once both appear, the cache is fully warm. To verify the zero-user-call promise, run a few /api/crypto/chart/* requests and grep the same log for 'pro-api.coingecko.com' immediately after — there must be NO new httpx lines correlated with user request timestamps; only the scheduler ticks (every 120s for prices, every 60min for long charts and rainbow) should appear.
rca of the issue: No bug to RCA — the architecture works as designed. The single ergonomic gap is the cold-start window of /api/crypto/chart/{coin}?days={30,90,365}: when a coin's long-period slot has not yet been populated by _prefetch_long_charts, the endpoint returns success=True with data=[] instead of the loading-state payload used by /api/crypto/rainbow. Recommended fix: in the chart endpoint, after looking up the cache slot, if the list is empty, return {success: False, error: 'Chart loading — please retry in a moment', coin_id, days, data: []} (HTTP 200) so the frontend can show a 'Loading…' state instead of an empty chart.
